creator: fullermd@futuresouth.com
text:
On Tue, Jun 15, 1999 at 10:37:10PM -0700, a little birdie told me
that dmmiller@cvzoom.net remarked
> 
...
>
> By the way, I had my kernel compiled with "-mpentium -O3", and used the
> same flags to "make world".  I also built fvwm2 and XFree86 3.3.3.1 with
> these flags.  It may be this very reason that FreeBSD is behaving like
> this, the fact that egcs may cause some unstable code as compared to the
> old gcc-2.7*.  Again, it doesn't happen very often, about once every two
> weeks.
> 
> >Fix:
> Stop using fvwm2.

Alternate fix:
Stop using -O3.
Last I heard there was known problems with -O above 1.
